cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this. The question is about what you should do when communicating with a pen pal, and the options are A, B, C, D. First, I need to look back at the passage to see what it says about safety and communication with pen pals. The relevant part is in the paragraph that starts with "There are things to remember when communication with pen pals, especially in the digital age..." Let me check that again. The passage says: "Make sure to share all letters and e-mails with your parents so they know you are safe. If something seems _ , stop writing letters. Never meet a pen pal in person unless a grown-up is with you." So the key points here are: share letters with parents, don't meet without a grown-up, and if something seems off, stop. The question is asking which of the options aligns with these safety measures. Looking at the options: A) Keep all the pen pal's letters and e-mails in private. That contradicts the passage's advice to share with parents. So A is probably wrong. B) Meet him/her with one of your classmates. The passage says never meet unless a grown-up is with you, not another kid. So B is incorrect. C) Be sure you are safe at any time. This is a general statement that encompasses the advice given in the passage (sharing with parents, not meeting alone, etc.). So C seems correct. D) Share whatever you experience with him/her. The passage emphasizes sharing with parents, not necessarily sharing everything with the pen pal. So D might not be right. The blank in the passage says "If something seems _ , stop writing letters." The word that fits there might be "suspicious" or "strange." But the question isn't about the blank.
